title: Charred Caramelized Mexican Corn Elote
description: A sweet and smoky Mexican dish featuring charred corn, caramelized onions, and dried apricots, all cooked to perfection in a claypot. This kosher-friendly recipe is perfect for adventurous eaters.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Grill the corn on the cob for 10-12 minutes, turning every 2 minutes, until charred on all sides.
In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion to the skillet and cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until caramelized.
Add the chopped dried apricots, brown sugar, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Stir in the lime juice and cook for an additional 30 seconds.
To serve, place a grilled ear of corn in a claypot, spoon the caramelized onion mixture over the top, and sprinkle with chopped cilantro.
